content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

September 21, 1978

Ruth Abraham
American Civil Liberties Union
of Southern California
633 South Shatto Place
Los Angeles, California 90005

Dear Mr. Abraham:

This letter is to confirm my telephone advice on
September 20, 1978, regarding the filing obligations of
the Southern California's American Civil Liberties Union
("ACLU") and the Gay Rights Chapter in efforts to defeat
Proposition 6 on the November 1978 ballot.

It is my understanding that the ACLU and the Gay Rights
Chapter each plan to spend $2,500 to contribute to the Stop
6 Committee. The ACLU has approved spending the $2,500 from
its general funds. The Gay Rights Chapter has also approved
spending $2,500 of its funds to oppose Proposition 6. It is
further my understanding that none of the funds to be spent
were earmarked for political purposes upon receipt.

Based on these facts, I advised you that the ACLU and
the Gay Rights Chapter will have the same reporting obligations.
Each will become a committee under Government Code Section
82013(c) when it contributes $5,000 or more for political purposes in a calendar year to or at the behest of other committees or candidates.

You also asked about possible reporting obligations in
connection with a fundraiser the Orange County Chapter of
the ACLU will hold on Saturday, September 23, 1978. As
stated on the invitations, the proceeds from the fundraiser
will go to the Orange County Chapter of the ACLU and to the
Orange County No on 6 Committee. I explained that since the
purchasers of the tickets were specifically notified that a
portion of their ticket price would go to the Orange County
No on 6 Committee, a portion of the purchase price is "earmarked" for political purposes and as such is a contribution.
(See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215(d), a copy of which is
enclosed.) To determine what portion of a ticket purchase is
a contribution, any reliable method of allocation may be used.

Ruth Abraham                                 September 21, 1978
Page Two


     One such method is to allocate based upon the pro rata share
of the money given to the Orange County No on 6 Committee. For example, if the Orange County ACLU received $3,000 from
the fundraiser and keeps $1,500 to cover its operating costs
and gives $1,500 to the Orange County No on 6 Committee, 50%
of the amount of the ticket purchases will be contributions.

     As I explained on the telephone, because the purchasers
have specific knowledge that the contribution will go to the
Orange County No on 6 Committee, the purchasers are the contributors to that committee and the Orange County ACLU will be
the intermediary*/ for the contributions it turns over to the
Orange County No on 6 Committee.

     As an intermediary, the Orange County ACLU is required,
under Government Code Section 84302, to advise the Orange
County No on 6 Committee of its name, address and function and
of the fact that it is acting as an intermediary for the
contributions. Additionally, as required by Government Code
Section 84302, the ACLU must provide the following information
to the Orange County No on 6 Committee for each contribution:
name of the contributor, address, date and amount of the contribution, occupation and the name of the employer, or if
self-employed, the principal place of business.




*/ Our conclusion would be different if the invitation had
not stated that the money would go specifically to the Orange
County No on 6 Committee. If there were just an indication that
the money would go to "defeat" or "oppose" Proposition 6, the
Orange County ACLU would have qualified as a recipient committee,
under Government Code Section 92013(a), when it received $500
in contributions. However, under the facts you presented, the
ACLU is only acting as a conduit for the contributions that have
been specifically noticed and earmarked to go to the Orange County
No on 6 Committee.

Ruth Abraham
Page Three

September 21, 1978

The Orange County No on 6 Committee will be required to
itemize any person who contributes a pro rata share of $50 or
more or whose cumulative amount of contributions totals $50
or more.

I hope this answers your questions. If you have any
others, please contact me or our Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division.

Sincerely,

Barbara Campbell
Counsel
Legal Division

Enclosure

cc: Meier Westreich
Orange County Chapter
of the ACLU
2009 N. Broadway
Santa Ana, California 92706
